signs.title: Road Signs
signs.empty: No signs in this category yet. Coming soon.

categoryPicker.freeLimitNotice: Only 15 free questions are available per type (text, photo, video). Upgrade to Premium to unlock all questions.

theoryQuiz.empty: No questions of this type yet.

psychMenu.title: Psychology
psychMenu.disclaimer: ⚠️ For a standard category B license, the psychological examination is usually NOT required — it's mandatory only for professional drivers (C/D), taxi drivers, emergency vehicle drivers, and those reinstating a revoked license. The exercises below are not real exam questions — they are original psychomotor practice exercises.

reaction.title: Reaction Speed
reaction.instructions: Press as fast as you can when the signal appears. If you press too early, you'll have to start over.
reaction.tooEarly: ⏳ You pressed too early! Wait for the signal.

coordination.title: Coordination Exercise
coordination.instructions: Drag the dot along the line, try not to go off it.
coordination.dragHint: Press and drag the dot to begin

piorkowski.title: Piórkowski Test
piorkowski.instructions: Tap the zone that lights up as fast as you can. There are {{rounds}} rounds.

krzyzowy.title: Krzyżowy Test
krzyzowy.instructions: Find the shape on the right matching the color on the left. Focus on the color, not the shape!

signal.title: Signal Test
signal.instructions: Press the button matching the color or sound signal. The time gets shorter each round — this is a stress test.

step1.title: Psychological examination
step1.note: Usually NOT REQUIRED for category B
step1.desc: Mandatory only for professional drivers (C/D), taxi, emergency vehicle drivers, or those reinstating a license. Check out the original practice tests.

step2.title: Theory course
step2.desc: Theoretical knowledge for the WORD exam. The app has 2000+ questions — text, photo, and video lessons, plus practice by category.

step3.title: Theory exam
step3.desc: Taken on a computer at an official WORD center. Continue your preparation in the app's Theory section.

step4.title: Practical driving course
step4.desc: The stage of learning to drive with an instructor — this app doesn't cover this part yet, arrange it with your instructor.

step5.title: Practical exam
step5.desc: Taken on real roads at a WORD center. Review road signs and traffic rules in the app.

profile.buyPremiumInstructions: Transfer {{amount}} zł via BLIK to {{phone}}, then enter your phone number here. The admin will review and activate premium.
profile.buyPremiumPendingSubtitle: Premium will activate automatically once your payment is confirmed. Usually reviewed quickly.

step1.title: 1. Make yourself and others safe
step1.text: Turn on your hazard lights. Move the car to the roadside if possible. Place the warning triangle — at least 50 meters within a built-up area, at least 100 meters outside it. If you get out of the car after dark, wear a reflective vest.

step2.title: 2. Call the emergency service
step2.text: Call 112 (free, works nationwide). If injuries are serious, you can also call 999 (ambulance).

step3.title: 3. What to say on the call
step3.text: Give the exact address or road number/kilometer, how many people are injured, their condition (conscious/unconscious, breathing or not), and your phone number. Don't hang up until the operator allows it.

step4.title: 4. First aid for the injured
step4.text: Place an unconscious but breathing person in the stable side (recovery) position, unless a back/neck injury is suspected. Stop heavy bleeding by pressing a clean cloth on it. Only remove someone from the car if there's a fire or explosion risk.

step5.title: 5. When you must always call the police
step5.text: The police must be called in cases of injury or death, significant property damage, if the parties can't agree between themselves, or if alcohol/drug use is suspected in one of the participants.

equipment.title: Mandatory Equipment

item1.title: Fire extinguisher (gaśnica)
item1.text: Must always be in the car. Must be functional, have valid certification, and be stored within quick reach.
item1.law: Regulation of the Minister of Infrastructure on technical conditions for vehicles

item2.title: Warning triangle (trójkąt ostrzegawczy)
item2.text: Must always be in the car. Must be certified and complete (all parts) — used to mark an accident scene on the road.
item2.law: Regulation of the Minister of Infrastructure on technical conditions for vehicles

item3.title: Reflective vest (kamizelka odblaskowa)
item3.text: Not required to be kept in the car. But if you get out of the car after dark outside a built-up area, you're legally considered a "pedestrian", and wearing a reflective element becomes MANDATORY.
item3.law: Road Traffic Law — rule on reflective elements for pedestrians

item4.title: First aid kit (apteczka)
item4.text: Currently (as of 2026) not legally mandatory for passenger cars — only recommended. The Ministry of Infrastructure is discussing making this mandatory in the future, so the rule may change.
item4.law: Current status — not yet mandatory (change pending)

equipment.fineNote: ⚠️ Missing mandatory equipment during a road check can result in a fine of 20 to 500 zł.

violations.title: Violations & Fines
violations.intro: The most common violations and the official fine tariff (as of 2026).

redLight.title: Running a red light
redLight.note: If this causes an accident, the fine starts at 1500 zł (500 + 1000 zł).

phone.title: Holding a phone while driving
phone.note: Even if you're briefly stopped at a light or in traffic, you're still considered the driver — the fine applies.

speed.title: Speeding
speed.note: See the full table below. From March 3, 2026, exceeding the limit by 50 km/h or more on a two-way road outside a built-up area results in automatic 3-month license suspension.

pedestrian.title: Failing to yield to a pedestrian (at a crossing)
pedestrian.note: The highest point value in the tariff — 15. If repeated within 2 years, the fine rises to 3000 zł.

drunkDriving.title: 🍺 Drunk driving — this is not a simple fine
drunkDriving.intro: For driving under the influence of alcohol, police don't issue a simple on-the-spot fine — the case always goes to court, and there are two tiers:
drunkDriving.tier1Text: Fine of 2500–30,000 zł, driving ban of 6 months to 3 years, 15 points.
drunkDriving.tier2Text: Up to 3 years imprisonment, driving ban of at least 3 years, monetary penalty of 5000–60,000 zł. If above 1.5‰ or if it causes an accident — vehicle confiscation is mandatory.
drunkDriving.note: From March 3, 2026, zero tolerance for alcohol applies to drivers on a probationary license (held less than 1 year).

pointsSystem.title: 📊 How the points system works
pointsSystem.text: The limit for drivers with a license under 1 year is 20 points, for experienced drivers — 24 points. Exceeding the limit can result in license revocation. Each point automatically expires 1 year after the fine is paid. From June 2026, points for serious violations like running a red light or phone use can no longer be reduced through a paid course.

courtRights.title: ⚖️ If you don't agree with the fine — your rights
courtRights.text: You're not obligated to accept the fine offered by the police — if you disagree, you have the right to take the case to court. But in court the fine may be higher (there's no legal upper limit, up to 30,000 zł in serious cases), and court costs will also fall on you. So think this decision through carefully.

feedback.title: Feedback & Suggestions
feedback.text: If you found a bug, have a suggestion, or a complaint — you can message the founder directly. Every message is reviewed personally.
